reason_for_decision: The article clearly separates identification from detection and tracking, explains cooperative and non-cooperative identification paths, and is strong enough to pass without revision.
strengths: The article consistently keeps detection, tracking, and identification as separate evidence stages.
It gives practical value by contrasting cooperative Remote ID-based identification with harder non-cooperative sensor fusion paths.
The discussion of confidence and workflow context keeps the topic operationally honest instead of overpromising.
summary_assessment: This is a strong Foundation article: it is readable, technically grounded, and useful enough to pass without revision.
